name: Alexander
desc: A creamy classic blending cognac, dark crème de cacao, and fresh cream.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice cubes
notes: Grated nutmeg on top is essential for the classic presentation.

name: Americano
desc: A bittersweet Italian aperitivo, the original Negroni predecessor.
steps: Pour Campari and vermouth over ice in a highball glass
Add a splash of soda water
Garnish with half orange slice and lemon zest
notes: Stir gently to preserve carbonation.

name: Angel Face
desc: A Prohibition-era equal-parts classic with gin, apricot brandy and Calvados.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice cubes
notes: Equal parts make this simple and perfectly balanced.

name: Aviation
desc: A sky-blue floral classic with maraschino and violet liqueur.
steps: Add all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Crème de violette gives the cocktail its signature sky-blue hue.

name: Between the Sheets
desc: A sassy sidecar variant combining rum, cognac and triple sec.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Balance is key — don't let the triple sec overwhelm.

name: Casino
desc: A vintage gin cocktail with maraschino and lemon, smooth and citrusy.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
Strain into chilled rocks glass with ice
notes: Old Tom Gin is sweeter than London Dry; don't substitute without adjusting sweetness.

name: Clover Club
desc: A pre-Prohibition pink frothy classic with raspberry and egg white.
steps: Double strain into chilled cocktail glass
notes: Dry shake first without ice to emulsify egg white properly.

name: Daiquiri
desc: Ernest Hemingway's favourite — a perfect trinity of rum, lime and sugar.
steps: Pour all ingredients into shaker with ice
notes: Use freshly squeezed lime juice for best results.

name: Derby
desc: A simple, elegant whiskey cocktail finished with fresh mint.
steps: Pour bourbon and bitters into mixing glass with ice
notes: Stir, don't shake — bourbon deserves a gentle touch.

name: Dry Martini
desc: The quintessential cocktail — gin and dry vermouth, stirred to perfection.
steps: Straight: Pour all ingredients into mixing glass with ice cubes
Garnish with olive or lemon zest
notes: Stir, never shake. The ratio of gin to vermouth is a matter of personal preference.

name: Fizz
desc: A refreshing gin sour topped with soda — crisp and lively.
steps: Shake gin, lemon juice and syrup with ice
Strain into highball glass with ice
notes: Pour soda gently to preserve bubbles.

name: Golden Dream
desc: A creamy dream of Galliano, triple sec, orange juice and cream.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Fresh cream makes this smooth and luxurious.

name: Hanky Panky
desc: Ada Coleman's legend — gin and sweet vermouth with a Fernet Branca twist.
steps: Pour all ingredients into mixing glass with ice
notes: Created by Ada Coleman at the Savoy. Fernet adds a unique herbal bitterness.

name: John Collins
desc: A tall, refreshing cousin of the Tom Collins made with bourbon.
steps: Pour whiskey, lemon juice and syrup into highball glass with ice
Stir gently and garnish with orange slice and cherry
notes: Tom Collins uses Old Tom Gin; John Collins uses bourbon.

name: Last Word
desc: A Prohibition-era equal-parts gem with Green Chartreuse and maraschino.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Equal parts, zero compromise. Don't skip the Chartreuse.

name: Manhattan
desc: The king of stirred cocktails — whiskey, sweet vermouth and bitters.
steps: Pour all ingredients into mixing glass with ice
Strain into chilled cocktail glass or rocks glass
notes: Rye whiskey gives more spice; bourbon gives more sweetness. Your choice.

name: Mary Pickford
desc: A silent-film-era classic with rum, pineapple and maraschino.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Named after the famous Hollywood actress of the 1920s.

name: Monkey's Lunch
desc: A tropical indulgence of rum, banana, coconut and coffee.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Coconut cream gives a tropical richness.

name: Negroni
desc: The perfect bitter-sweet Italian classic — equal parts, infinite depth.
steps: Pour all ingredients into mixing glass with ice cubes
Strain into chilled old fashioned glass with ice cubes
notes: Stir, don't shake. Serve with one large ice cube if possible.

name: Old Fashioned
desc: The original cocktail — whiskey, sugar, bitters. Nothing more, nothing less.
steps: Place sugar cube in old fashioned glass and saturate with bitters
Add a few dashes of water and muddle until dissolved
Fill glass with ice cubes and add whiskey
Garnish with orange slice and cocktail cherry
notes: The oldest cocktail still standing. Keep it simple.

name: Porto Flip
desc: A velvety after-dinner flip with port wine, brandy and egg.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Use fresh egg yolk for the richest texture.

name: Bacardi Cocktail
desc: Legally must be made with Bacardi — a court ruled in 1936.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: A New York Supreme Court ruling in 1936 declared this must be made with Bacardi rum.

name: Bellini
desc: Venice's favourite aperitivo — white peach purée and sparkling Prosecco.
steps: Pour peach purée into chilled champagne flute
notes: Created by Giuseppe Cipriani at Harry's Bar, Venice. White peach only.

name: Black Russian
desc: Simple, dark and smooth — vodka and coffee liqueur over ice.
steps: Pour ingredients into old fashioned glass filled with ice cubes
notes: Add cream to make a White Russian.

name: Bloody Mary
desc: The ultimate savoury cocktail — vodka, tomato and a kick of spice.
steps: Pour all ingredients into highball glass with ice
Roll between two glasses to mix
Garnish with celery stalk and lemon wedge
notes: Roll (don't shake) for the smoothest blend of flavours.

name: Caipirinha
desc: Brazil's national cocktail — cachaça, lime and sugar, muddled to perfection.
steps: Cut lime into wedges and place in rocks glass
notes: Only cachaça is authentic. Cut the lime into 8 wedges for maximum juice.

name: Champagne Cocktail
desc: Elegant and classic — a sugar cube soaked in bitters, topped with Champagne.
steps: Place sugar cube at bottom of flute and saturate with bitters
Garnish with orange slice and cherry
notes: The sugar cube slowly dissolves, releasing bitters as you drink.

name: Corpse Reviver #2
desc: A classic hair-of-the-dog reviver with absinthe rinse.
steps: Rinse chilled cocktail glass with absinthe
Pour remaining ingredients into shaker with ice
notes: Four of these taken in quick succession will unrevive the corpse again.

name: Cosmopolitan
desc: Sex and the City's drink — cranberry-pink, citrusy and eternally stylish.
steps: Add all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
Garnish with lime wedge or flamed orange peel
notes: The cranberry should tint the drink pink, not red.

name: Cuba Libre
desc: Free Cuba in a glass — rum, cola and a squeeze of lime.
steps: Build into highball glass filled with ice
Stir gently, garnish with lime wedge
notes: Born during the Spanish-American War. Use quality cola for best results.

name: Dark 'n' Stormy
desc: Bermuda's national drink — Gosling's dark rum over ginger beer.
notes: Traditionally made with Gosling's Black Seal rum. Float the rum on top for the dramatic dark-over-light presentation.

name: French Connection
desc: Two French spirits in harmony — cognac and Amaretto.
steps: Pour ingredients into old fashioned glass filled with ice
notes: Simple two-ingredient cocktail of great elegance.

name: French Martini
desc: A frothy pink 90s classic with Chambord and pineapple.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Shake hard for a foamy top from the pineapple juice.

name: Godfather
desc: A smooth Scotch and Amaretto duo — strong and unmistakably smooth.
steps: Pour ingredients into old fashioned glass filled with ice
notes: The Godmother uses vodka instead of Scotch.

name: Grasshopper
desc: A luscious mint-and-cream after-dinner classic.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Equal parts for balance. Best served as a digestif.

name: Harvey Wallbanger
desc: A funky 70s classic — screwdriver with a Galliano float.
steps: Pour vodka and orange juice into highball glass with ice
Garnish with orange slice and cherry
notes: The Galliano float is what makes this different from a Screwdriver.

name: Hemingway Special
desc: Papa Doble — a grapefruit daiquiri variant, created for Hemingway's diabetes.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: No sugar — Hemingway was diabetic. Use good quality white rum.

name: Horse's Neck
desc: A tall, spiralling lemon peel cocktail with brandy and ginger ale.
steps: Build in highball glass with ice
Cut long lemon peel spiral and drape over rim
notes: The long lemon peel spiral gives this cocktail its name.

name: Irish Coffee
desc: A warm welcome — Irish whiskey, hot coffee and softly whipped cream.
steps: Pre-heat Irish coffee glass with hot water
Float lightly whipped cream on top
notes: Drink through the cream — don't stir it in.

name: Kir
desc: A French aperitif classic — crème de cassis in dry white wine.
steps: Pour crème de cassis into wine glass
notes: Use Bourgogne Aligoté for the most traditional version.

name: Kir Royal
desc: The royal version of Kir — crème de cassis topped with Champagne.
steps: Pour crème de cassis into chilled flute
notes: Do not stir — let the Champagne do the mixing.

name: Long Island Iced Tea
desc: Deceivingly strong — five spirits disguised as iced tea.
steps: Pour all spirits and lemon juice into highball glass with ice
notes: The cola is for colour only — go easy with it.

name: Lemon Drop Martini
desc: A lip-puckering citrus martini with a sugar-rimmed glass.
steps: Coat rim of chilled martini glass with sugar
Pour all ingredients into shaker with ice
notes: Adjust syrup to balance tartness to your taste.

name: Lynchburg Lemonade
desc: Jack Daniel's hometown pride — whiskey, triple sec and lemonade.
steps: Build in highball glass with ice
notes: A crowd-pleasing summer drink from Lynchburg, Tennessee.

name: Mai Tai
desc: Trader Vic's ultimate tiki classic — rum, orgeat and citrus.
steps: Pour all ingredients into shaker with ice
Strain into rocks glass with crushed ice
Garnish with lime wheel and mint sprig
notes: Mai Tai means 'out of this world' in Tahitian.

name: Margarita
desc: Mexico's gift to the world — tequila, lime and triple sec.
steps: Rub rim of chilled cocktail glass with lime and dip in salt
Pour all ingredients into shaker with ice
notes: 100% agave tequila makes a noticeably better margarita.

name: Mimosa
desc: The brunch classic — equal parts Champagne and orange juice.
steps: Pour chilled orange juice into flute
notes: Always add juice first to preserve carbonation.

name: Mint Julep
desc: The Kentucky Derby's official drink — bourbon, mint and crushed ice.
steps: Muddle mint, sugar and water in silver julep cup
Stir well and garnish with mint bouquet
notes: Serve in a silver or pewter cup for tradition. The frost that forms on the outside is part of the experience.

name: Mojito
desc: Cuba's most famous export — rum, mint, lime and soda.
steps: Muddle mint gently with sugar in highball glass
Stir gently and garnish with mint sprig
notes: Don't over-muddle the mint — bruise, don't shred.

name: Moscow Mule
desc: The copper mug classic — vodka, ginger beer and lime.
steps: Fill copper mug or highball glass with ice
Stir gently and garnish with lime slice
notes: Invented in 1941. Traditionally served in copper mugs.

name: Piña Colada
desc: Puerto Rico's national cocktail — rum, pineapple and coconut cream.
steps: Blend all ingredients with a cup of crushed ice until smooth
Garnish with pineapple wedge and cherry
notes: Blend smooth, not icy. A slice of fresh pineapple on the rim is classic.

name: Planter's Punch
desc: A timeless Caribbean long drink — rum, citrus and grenadine.
steps: Pour all ingredients into shaker with ice
Strain into highball glass with ice
notes: One of sour, two of sweet, three of strong, four of weak — the original rum punch formula.

name: Rusty Nail
desc: Scotch and Drambuie — two Scottish spirits, one unforgettable drink.
steps: Pour ingredients into old fashioned glass with ice
notes: Adjust the Drambuie ratio to your desired sweetness.

name: Sea Breeze
desc: A fruity, refreshing cooler with cranberry and grapefruit.
steps: Build in highball glass with ice
notes: Fresh grapefruit juice is a big upgrade over bottled.

name: Sex on the Beach
desc: A fruity 80s classic — vodka, peach schnapps, cranberry and OJ.
steps: Build in highball glass with ice
notes: One of the most ordered cocktails globally since the 1980s.

name: Singapore Sling
desc: Raffles Hotel's iconic cocktail — gin, cherry liqueur and tropical juices.
steps: Pour all ingredients into shaker with ice
Strain into highball glass with ice
Garnish with pineapple slice and cocktail cherry
notes: Created at the Long Bar of Raffles Hotel Singapore circa 1915.

name: Sling
desc: The original gin sling — simple, balanced and refreshingly tall.
steps: Shake gin, cherry heering, lemon juice and syrup with ice
Strain into highball glass with ice
notes: The Singapore Sling is a famous elaboration of this classic.

name: Stinger
desc: An after-dinner classic of cognac and white crème de menthe.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: A classic digestif — the mint refreshes the palate after a rich meal.

name: Tequila Sunrise
desc: A sunrise in a glass — tequila, orange juice and grenadine.
steps: Pour tequila into highball glass with ice
Slowly pour grenadine down side of glass
Do not stir — let it sink for the sunrise effect
notes: Do not stir! The grenadine sinks to create the sunrise gradient.

name: Zombie
desc: Trader Vic's most famous creation — a powerful tiki blend with multiple rums.
steps: Pour all ingredients into shaker with ice
Strain into highball glass with ice
notes: Trader Vic's original formula is a trade secret. Guests were limited to two per sitting.

name: Airmail
desc: A light and bubbly rum sour topped with Champagne.
steps: Shake rum, lime juice and honey syrup with ice
notes: Honey syrup: 2:1 honey to water, warm to dissolve.

name: Amaretto Sour
desc: A modern sour revival — silky amaretto with egg white foam.
steps: Add ice and shake again vigorously
Double strain into rocks glass with ice
Garnish with Angostura bitters dots on foam
notes: The bourbon tempers the sweetness of the amaretto — don't skip it.

name: Aperol Spritz
desc: Italy's most exported aperitivo ritual — Aperol, Prosecco and soda.
steps: Fill large wine glass with ice
notes: 3-2-1 ratio: Prosecco, Aperol, soda. Always wine glass, always orange.

name: Barracuda
desc: A tropical fizz of rum, Galliano and pineapple, finished with Prosecco.
steps: Shake rum, Galliano, pineapple and lime with ice
Top with a splash of Prosecco
notes: The Galliano adds a herbal vanilla note that elevates this tropical fizz.

name: Bramble
desc: Dick Bradsell's 1984 masterpiece — gin sour with blackberry liqueur.
steps: Fill rocks glass with crushed ice
Shake gin, lemon juice and syrup with ice
Drizzle crème de mure over top
Garnish with lemon slice and fresh blackberries
notes: Created by Dick Bradsell in London, 1984. The crème de mure creates a beautiful marble effect.

name: Cloister
desc: A herbaceous gin cocktail with grapefruit and chartreuse.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Yellow Chartreuse is milder than Green — suits this citrus combination perfectly.

name: Dirty Martini
desc: The brine-lover's martini — vodka with olive brine.
steps: Pour all ingredients into mixing glass with ice
notes: Adjust olive brine to taste. Extra dirty = more brine.

name: Eggnog
desc: Christmas in a glass — rich, creamy and spiced.
notes: For a crowd, scale up and batch in advance. Nutmeg on top is non-negotiable.

name: Espresso Martini
desc: Dick Bradsell's 1980s caffeine kick — vodka, espresso and coffee liqueur.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Use freshly brewed espresso — it makes the frothy crema on top.

name: Flirtini
desc: A flirtatious pink fizz of vodka, Cointreau and pineapple.
steps: Shake vodka, Cointreau and pineapple juice with ice
notes: Don't skip the Champagne — it lifts the whole cocktail.

name: Garibaldi
desc: Simple and sunshine-bright — Campari and fluffy orange juice.
steps: Pour Campari into highball glass with ice
Top with freshly juiced orange juice
notes: Juice the orange just before serving — the fluffy texture is key.

name: Gimlet
desc: A British Navy essential — gin and lime juice, tart and clean.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Traditionally made with Rose's lime cordial, but fresh lime is far superior.

name: Illegal
desc: A smoky twist on the margarita with mezcal and triple sec.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: The mezcal smokiness and rum intensity give this a daring character.

name: Naked and Famous
desc: An equal-parts modern classic — mezcal, Aperol, Yellow Chartreuse and lime.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Created by Joaquín Simó at Death & Co, NYC, 2011. Equal parts, maximum impact.

name: Paloma
desc: Mexico drinks this more than margaritas — tequila and grapefruit soda.
steps: Salt the rim of highball glass
notes: Use Jarritos Toronja or Squirt for authenticity.

name: Paper Plane
desc: A modern equal-parts classic — bourbon, Aperol, Amaro and lemon.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
notes: Created by Sam Ross at Milk & Honey, NYC. Equal parts perfection.

name: Pisco Sour
desc: Peru's national pride — pisco, lime, syrup and a fluffy egg white foam.
steps: Dry shake all ingredients without ice
Add ice and shake again vigorously
Dot bitters on foam and drag with toothpick
notes: The bitters design on the foam is traditional — don't skip it.

name: Pornstar Martini
desc: The UK's most popular cocktail — passion fruit, vanilla vodka and Champagne on the side.
steps: Shake vodka, passion fruit liqueur, purée, lime and syrup with ice
Double strain into chilled cocktail glass
Serve Champagne in shot glass on the side
notes: Sip the cocktail and take the Champagne shot. Created by Douglas Ankrah in London.

name: Russian Spring Punch
desc: Dick Bradsell's effervescent vodka and blackcurrant punch.
steps: Shake vodka, cassis, lemon juice and syrup with ice
Strain into highball glass with ice
Garnish with fresh berries and lemon slice
notes: Another Dick Bradsell classic — effortlessly elegant and delicious.

name: Southside
desc: A minty gin sour — gin, lime, sugar and fresh mint.
steps: Double strain into chilled cocktail glass
notes: Double strain to remove mint fragments for a clean presentation.

name: Spritz Veneziano
desc: Venice's afternoon ritual — Select or Aperol with Prosecco.
steps: Fill large wine glass with ice
Garnish with olive or orange slice
notes: In Venice they use Select Aperitivo, not Aperol — try both!

name: Tommy's Margarita
desc: Julio Bermejo's agave-focused update — tequila, lime and agave nectar.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
Strain into rocks glass with ice
notes: No salt rim, no triple sec. Let the agave shine. Created at Tommy's Mexican Restaurant, San Francisco.

name: Tzar's Fizz
desc: A Russian-inspired fizz with violet and lemon topped with Champagne.
steps: Shake gin, violette, lemon and syrup with ice
notes: The violet turns this a stunning purple-blue colour.

name: Vesper
desc: James Bond's original martini — gin, vodka and Lillet, shaken not stirred.
steps: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker with ice
Garnish with large thin lemon peel
notes: Created by Ian Fleming in Casino Royale. Shaken, not stirred — as specified by Bond himself.
